steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank fills in a book title within the noun phrase “her 2019 novel ______.” This noun phrase is the subject of the verb “offers.” The title directly names and identifies which novel is being discussed.

steps.title: Match the structure to correct punctuation
steps.content:
In standard English, when a title directly follows and identifies a noun, no punctuation separates them. The construction is simply noun + title with no mark between. The other three options add unnecessary punctuation: a comma or em dash interrupts the subject from its verb, and a colon introduces unwarranted formality for a straightforward identification.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
